description:
When any CLI command mutates .ao/ state files (tasks, requirements, workflow state, daemon config), automatically commit those changes and push to origin/main. This prevents state drift when the daemon or other agents rebase or merge, which can cause loss of local state changes.

Scope:
- Add a post-mutation hook in FileServiceHub (or at the CLI command layer) that detects .ao/ file changes
- Stage only .ao/ files, commit with a conventional message like "chore(ao): sync task/requirement state"
- Push to origin/main
- Should be fast and non-blocking — fire-and-forget or background
- Must handle: no remote configured, push conflicts (pull --rebase first), dirty working tree outside .ao/
- Should respect a config flag to disable if needed (e.g. ao.auto_commit_state = false)
title: Auto-commit and push .ao/ state changes on CLI mutations
